title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: One Killed, at Least 63 Injured by Iran Strike on Airport in Kuwait
stories.summary:
One Killed, at Least 63 Injured by Iran Strike on Airport in Kuwait. Kuwaiti officials said on Wednesday that at least one person was killed and 63 injured by an Iranian missile and drone attack on the Kuwait International Airport.

The post One Killed, at Least 63 Injured by Iran Strike on Airport in Kuwait appeared first on Breitbart .

Al Jazeera: Kuwait labels Iran attack ‘heinous aggression’. Al Jazeera reports that kuwait’s defence ministry has labelled an attack on the country’s international airport as ‘heinous Iranian aggression’.

links.excerpt: Kuwait’s defence ministry has labelled an attack on the country’s international airport as ‘heinous Iranian aggression’.

stories.title: US House passes Iran war powers resolution in rare pushback against Trump
stories.summary:
US House passes Iran war powers resolution in rare pushback against Trump. Four Republicans allied with Democrats to pass bill to rein in Trump, though it is likely to face a presidential veto.

BBC: US House votes to rein in Trump's war powers in rebuke over Iran.

BBC reports that in the 215-208 vote, four Republicans joined Democrats to pass the measure, which had failed in three previous attempts.

stories.title: Longtime ‘60 Minutes’ Anchor Fired After Attacks On New Leadership
stories.summary:
Longtime ‘60 Minutes’ Anchor Fired After Attacks On New Leadership. CBS News fired “60 Minutes” correspondent Scott Pelley on Tuesday, just one day after he accused editor-in-chief Bari Weiss of attempting to “murder” the long-running show.

Nick Bilton, the tech journalist and filmmaker recently named the new boss of “60 Minutes,” told Pelley that he was being fired after he aggressively “hijacked” a staff meeting ... BBC: CBS News fires correspondent Scott Pelley from 60 Minutes.

BBC reports that the veteran host had accused leadership of "murdering" the news show in a confrontation at a staff meeting this week.

stories.title: Foreign nationals among at least 21 killed in Delhi fire
stories.summary:
Foreign nationals among at least 21 killed in Delhi fire. Many victims were South Asians who had travelled to India for treatment or to accompany relatives.

Al Jazeera: Deadly fire rips through New Delhi hotel.

Al Jazeera reports that a fire has ripped through a hotel in New Delhi, killing at least 21 people including 18 foreign nationals.

stories.title: Unions condemn Trump order making federal workers ‘at-will’ employees
stories.summary:
Unions condemn Trump order making federal workers ‘at-will’ employees. President Donald Trump signed an executive order Wednesday making it easier to remove thousands of senior federal employees, prompting backlash from federal worker unions that accused the administration of attempting to politicize the civil service.

The order reclassifies roughly 8,000 federal positions into a new category known as “Schedule Policy/Career,” affecting employees with significant policymaking […]. Breitbart: Trump Signs EOs Bolstering Customs Enforcement, Federal Workforce Accountability.

Breitbart reports that wASHINGTON—President Donald Trump signed a pair of executive orders on Wednesday to bolster customs enforcement and bring more accountability to the federal workforce. The post Trump Signs EOs Bolstering Customs Enforcement, Federal Workforce Accountability appeared first on Breitba.

stories.title: David Pocock highlights ‘bonkers’ situation facing podcasts over gambling ad opt-out – video
stories.summary:
David Pocock highlights ‘bonkers’ situation facing podcasts over gambling ad opt-out – video.

Live-read sponsorships on podcasts would be included in proposed online advertising restrictions to gambling ads, officials confirmed in Senate estimates, which independent senator David Pocock said would be a 'bonkers' situation requiring podcasters to upload multiple versions of a podcast.

Under the proposed restrictions, streaming platforms can only have gambling ads for users who are over 18 and have logged in and must allow users to opt out of seeing or hearing such advertisements, in or.

stories.title: If you want to run your first marathon in your 50s, it helps to be chased by zombies
stories.summary:
If you want to run your first marathon in your 50s, it helps to be chased by zombies. When Ben Elton didn’t distract from the pain of moving my body, I found the perfect solution – the interactive smartphone game Zombies, Run!

At 56, I am running my first marathon, an old, fat, bald dad surrounded by millennials in body-hugging Lycra and smiles that look AI-generated.

But I am ahead of them. For they are only competing for positions and personal bests, and I am being chased by zombies.

The black dog of depression hit me around the time of my last birthday. I didn’t.

stories.title: Studio Display XDR review: Apple’s pro display shines very brightly
stories.summary:
Studio Display XDR review: Apple’s pro display shines very brightly.

Crisp 27in 5K Mac monitor is packed with features and some of the best HDR performance you can get for work or play Apple’s new 27in Studio Display XDR is its best monitor yet, with an exceptionally bright and gorgeous 5K screen that wants to be the pro display for Mac-wielding content creators everywhere, with a price tag to match.

stories.title: Gatchalian to lead Duterte impeachment trial as acting Senate president
stories.summary:
Gatchalian to lead Duterte impeachment trial as acting Senate president. MANILA, Philippines — Senator Sherwin Gatchalian on Thursday said he iwill preside over Vice President Sara Duterte’s impeachment trial as the acting Senate president after a recent leadership change in the upper chamber.

“Well under the rules since ang Senate president pro tempore assumes the acting president, tayo ang magp-preside ng impeachment,” said Gatchalian. Asked.

Rappler: Post-Senate shakeup sees two sides still battling for control. Rappler reports that acting Senate President Sherwin Gatchalian reaffirms the legitimacy of the latest leadership change in the upper chamber.

Alan Peter Cayetano insists he remains in control of the Senate.

stories.title: PPI holds meeting, forum, media awards June 4-6
stories.summary:
PPI holds meeting, forum, media awards June 4-6. MANILA, Philippines — On its 62nd year, the Philippine Press Institute (PPI)—the national association of newspapers and online publications—continues to recognize initiatives that benefit not just its members but the communities they serve.

As digital innovations present both challenges and opportunities for the press, PPI tackles this shift with twin goals—maintaining credibility amid a wave.

stories.summary:

TAGBILARAN CITY, BOHOL, Philippines — The Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) has urged resort owners and occupants along the coastline of Siquijor province to comply with foreshore regulations and legal easement requirements, warning that violations could result in penalties and legal action.

The DENR assessed 107 coastal resorts across the island province, based.

stories.title: Sen. Padilla, DILG chief Remulla figure in Senate scuffle
stories.summary:
Sen. Padilla, DILG chief Remulla figure in Senate scuffle. MANILA, Philippines — Tensions at the Senate have turned physical on Thursday as Sen. Robinhood Padilla and Interior Sec.

Jonvic Remulla shoved each other right inside the Senate building.

The physical altercation occurred while Padilla—along with Sen. Pia Cayetano—guided the 18 supposedly former Marines, who were invited as resource persons in Thursday’s Senate blue ribbon. Philippine Daily Inquirer: Cayetano, allies show up at Senate.

Philippine Daily Inquirer reports that mANILA, Philippines — After three days of absence, Sen. Alan Peter Cayetano and his allies arrived at the Senate on Thursday. He was joined by Sen. Robin Padilla and Sen. Pia Cayetano.

Other members of the bloc are also anticipated to arrive. Prior to the Senate reorganization on Wednesday, Pia Cayetano set a blue ribbon.

stories.summary:

MANILA, Philippines — The House of Representatives on Wednesday passed on final reading a bill barring second degree relatives from holding public office simultaneously, although minority lawmakers viewed the approved measure as a sham attempt at preventing dynastic clans from continuing to dominate politics.
